name: Cannoli Alla Siciliana
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Wine, Marsala, Burgundy or Chablis
3 tablespoons Orange peel, candied and chopped
recipeInstructions:
To make cannoli shells, you will need 3 or 4 metal tubes about 7-inches long and 1 1/8 inches in diameter. Do not solder the edges. (Old aluminum chairs cut into pieces work well.)
1. Combine flour, shortening, sugar, and salt, gradually adding wine until a firm dough forms. Form into a ball, cover with cloth, and let stand about 1 hour.
2. Cut dough in half and roll out one portion into a thin sheet about 1/4 inch thick. Cut into 4-inch squares.
3. Place a metal tube diagonally across each square from one point to another, wrapping dough around the tube by overlapping points and sealing with egg white.
4. Heat oil in a large deep pan for frying. Drop shells gently into hot oil until golden brown. Remove from oil, let cool, and carefully remove shells from tubes.
5. Repeat until all shells are made.
Filling:
1. Mix ricotta thoroughly with sifted dry ingredients (sugar, salt). Add vanilla and fruit peel; mix well. A small amount of grated pistachio may be added if desired.
2. Chill in the refrigerator before filling shells.
3. Fill cold cannoli shells, smoothing filling at each end. Decorate ends with glace cherry pieces and sprinkle confectioners sugar on shells.
4. Refrigerate until ready to serve. Best when filled just before company arrives.
